text: Ingrid Laane. Due to the coronavirus crisis, the Technology, Media and Telecoms sector (TMT) will shrink by 6 per cent in 2020, followed by a partial recovery. Advertising agencies are taking heavy blows as clients act more cautiously. Printers are seeing advertising print jobs disappear, also because events and trade fairs have been cancelled. These branches need more than a year to recover from the hit.

The outbreak of the coronavirus crisis has turned the world upside down and pushed the economy into a deep recession. text: Next year the IT sector will be well above pre-corona levels. The outbreak of the coronavirus crisis has turned the world upside down and pushed the economy into a deep recession. The Dutch economy shrank by 8.5 per cent in the second quarter compared with the previous quarter. This drop is the result of sharply lower household consumption, declining investment and less foreign trade. For 2020 as a whole ABN AMRO [expects](https://insights.abnamro.nl/2020/08/recordkrimp-nederlandse-economie-in-tweede-kwartaal/) the Dutch economy to shrink by 5.2 per cent.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: The outbreak of the coronavirus crisis has turned the world upside down and pushed the economy into a deep recession. The Dutch economy shrank by 8.5 per cent in the second quarter compared with the previous quarter. This drop is the result of sharply lower household consumption, declining investment and less foreign trade. For 2020 as a whole ABN AMRO [expects](https://insights.abnamro.nl/2020/08/recordkrimp-nederlandse-economie-in-tweede-kwartaal/) the Dutch economy to shrink by 5.2 per cent. Although the TMT sector has not been hit the hardest by the crisis, the effects of the above developments are also being felt here.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: The Dutch economy shrank by 8.5 per cent in the second quarter compared with the previous quarter. This drop is the result of sharply lower household consumption, declining investment and less foreign trade. For 2020 as a whole ABN AMRO [expects](https://insights.abnamro.nl/2020/08/recordkrimp-nederlandse-economie-in-tweede-kwartaal/) the Dutch economy to shrink by 5.2 per cent. Although the TMT sector has not been hit the hardest by the crisis, the effects of the above developments are also being felt here. In 2020 ABN AMRO therefore expects a 6 per cent contraction of the TMT sector.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: This drop is the result of sharply lower household consumption, declining investment and less foreign trade. For 2020 as a whole ABN AMRO [expects](https://insights.abnamro.nl/2020/08/recordkrimp-nederlandse-economie-in-tweede-kwartaal/) the Dutch economy to shrink by 5.2 per cent. Although the TMT sector has not been hit the hardest by the crisis, the effects of the above developments are also being felt here. In 2020 ABN AMRO therefore expects a 6 per cent contraction of the TMT sector. However, the differences between the underlying branches are large, as are the recovery expectations.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: For 2020 as a whole ABN AMRO [expects](https://insights.abnamro.nl/2020/08/recordkrimp-nederlandse-economie-in-tweede-kwartaal/) the Dutch economy to shrink by 5.2 per cent. Although the TMT sector has not been hit the hardest by the crisis, the effects of the above developments are also being felt here. In 2020 ABN AMRO therefore expects a 6 per cent contraction of the TMT sector. However, the differences between the underlying branches are large, as are the recovery expectations. This reinforces the long-standing picture of a sector with two faces.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: Although the TMT sector has not been hit the hardest by the crisis, the effects of the above developments are also being felt here. In 2020 ABN AMRO therefore expects a 6 per cent contraction of the TMT sector. However, the differences between the underlying branches are large, as are the recovery expectations. This reinforces the long-standing picture of a sector with two faces. On the one hand, there is the IT sector, which is holding up relatively well in the coronavirus crisis due to increased demand for digital services as a result of working from home.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: In 2020 ABN AMRO therefore expects a 6 per cent contraction of the TMT sector. However, the differences between the underlying branches are large, as are the recovery expectations. This reinforces the long-standing picture of a sector with two faces. On the one hand, there is the IT sector, which is holding up relatively well in the coronavirus crisis due to increased demand for digital services as a result of working from home. Although a possible blow later this year cannot be ruled out because clients may still run into difficulties, the expectation is that the sector will also recover quickly.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: However, the differences between the underlying branches are large, as are the recovery expectations. This reinforces the long-standing picture of a sector with two faces. On the one hand, there is the IT sector, which is holding up relatively well in the coronavirus crisis due to increased demand for digital services as a result of working from home. Although a possible blow later this year cannot be ruled out because clients may still run into difficulties, the expectation is that the sector will also recover quickly. This is due to the highly digitalised Dutch economy, which received an extra push from the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: This reinforces the long-standing picture of a sector with two faces. On the one hand, there is the IT sector, which is holding up relatively well in the coronavirus crisis due to increased demand for digital services as a result of working from home. Although a possible blow later this year cannot be ruled out because clients may still run into difficulties, the expectation is that the sector will also recover quickly. This is due to the highly digitalised Dutch economy, which received an extra push from the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis. This extra push means the crisis may even deliver structural benefits for the IT sector in the longer term.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: On the one hand, there is the IT sector, which is holding up relatively well in the coronavirus crisis due to increased demand for digital services as a result of working from home. Although a possible blow later this year cannot be ruled out because clients may still run into difficulties, the expectation is that the sector will also recover quickly. This is due to the highly digitalised Dutch economy, which received an extra push from the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis. This extra push means the crisis may even deliver structural benefits for the IT sector in the longer term. If companies do more to facilitate working from home and if services initially digitalised out of necessity, such as online education and digital healthcare provision, gain a firm foothold, the sector will reach structurally higher peaks.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: Although a possible blow later this year cannot be ruled out because clients may still run into difficulties, the expectation is that the sector will also recover quickly. This is due to the highly digitalised Dutch economy, which received an extra push from the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis. This extra push means the crisis may even deliver structural benefits for the IT sector in the longer term. If companies do more to facilitate working from home and if services initially digitalised out of necessity, such as online education and digital healthcare provision, gain a firm foothold, the sector will reach structurally higher peaks. On the other hand, there are publishers and printers, which have long faced a shrinking market and have taken an extra hit due to the coronavirus crisis.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: This is due to the highly digitalised Dutch economy, which received an extra push from the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis. This extra push means the crisis may even deliver structural benefits for the IT sector in the longer term. If companies do more to facilitate working from home and if services initially digitalised out of necessity, such as online education and digital healthcare provision, gain a firm foothold, the sector will reach structurally higher peaks. On the other hand, there are publishers and printers, which have long faced a shrinking market and have taken an extra hit due to the coronavirus crisis. The advertising industry is the worst affected, having seen a lot of turnover disappear due to sharply declining media spending.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: This extra push means the crisis may even deliver structural benefits for the IT sector in the longer term. If companies do more to facilitate working from home and if services initially digitalised out of necessity, such as online education and digital healthcare provision, gain a firm foothold, the sector will reach structurally higher peaks. On the other hand, there are publishers and printers, which have long faced a shrinking market and have taken an extra hit due to the coronavirus crisis. The advertising industry is the worst affected, having seen a lot of turnover disappear due to sharply declining media spending. Driven by a strong recovery in IT, by far the largest branch within TMT, the sector as a whole will recover relatively quickly.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: If companies do more to facilitate working from home and if services initially digitalised out of necessity, such as online education and digital healthcare provision, gain a firm foothold, the sector will reach structurally higher peaks. On the other hand, there are publishers and printers, which have long faced a shrinking market and have taken an extra hit due to the coronavirus crisis. The advertising industry is the worst affected, having seen a lot of turnover disappear due to sharply declining media spending. Driven by a strong recovery in IT, by far the largest branch within TMT, the sector as a whole will recover relatively quickly. ABN AMRO expects growth of 4 per cent in 2021.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: On the other hand, there are publishers and printers, which have long faced a shrinking market and have taken an extra hit due to the coronavirus crisis. The advertising industry is the worst affected, having seen a lot of turnover disappear due to sharply declining media spending. Driven by a strong recovery in IT, by far the largest branch within TMT, the sector as a whole will recover relatively quickly. ABN AMRO expects growth of 4 per cent in 2021. The sector will not yet be back at the level before the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis, but it will make a good step in that direction.

metadata.source_slug: IT-branche in 2021 al ruim boven pre-coronaniveaus
metadata.source_title: IT sector well above pre-corona levels in 2021

text: The advertising industry is the worst affected, having seen a lot of turnover disappear due to sharply declining media spending. Driven by a strong recovery in IT, by far the largest branch within TMT, the sector as a whole will recover relatively quickly. ABN AMRO expects growth of 4 per cent in 2021. The sector will not yet be back at the level before the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis, but it will make a good step in that direction. Due to uncertainty about how the coronavirus will develop, these expectations are surrounded by much uncertainty.

The sector will not yet be back at the level before the outbreak of the coronavirus crisis, but it will make a good step in that direction.
